scene: 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: Single-card-leads layout is sound but ships rather than delights: an absent-feature explainer ('bundle'), a competing color story (three greens vs a blue CTA), and no coverage visual.
fix_recommendation: Expand the candidate-plans section by default or add a coverage map/summary band so the lower viewport isn't dead space; align the primary CTA color with the readiness badges so one accent owns 'ready -> act'.

scene: 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: Screenshot dominated by empty lower two-thirds; the hover cursor occludes the CTA label; three stacked greens fight a blue CTA.
fix_recommendation: Expand candidate-plans (or add a coverage map) to fill the lower viewport; reposition the hover cursor off the CTA glyphs.

scene: 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: Hover cursor sits on top of the 'Create solicitation' button text rather than cleanly indicating it; no distinct active/hover state.
fix_recommendation: Offset the recorded cursor to the button's edge so the label stays readable, and ensure the hovered CTA shows a distinct hover state (shadow/darken).

scene: 2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: Pre-seeded coverage panel is present and provenance-captioned but under-emphasized; arms/wards labeled three inconsistent ways, yielding a partial 5-second read.
fix_recommendation: Give the Coverage panel primary visual weight (taller map, single clear arm legend 'Intervention = Attakar / Comparison = Gura'); collapse the three naming rows into one.

scene: 2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: Three equal-weight empty optional sections (Description, Application Questions, Evaluation Criteria) below the one seeded artifact flatten hierarchy so the seeded content doesn't read as the point.
fix_recommendation: Demote/collapse empty optional sections; visually anchor the pre-seeded Scope + Coverage as the filled 'starting point'.

scene: 2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: The load-bearing Mapbox panel renders tiny and cramped relative to the empty fields above it.
fix_recommendation: Enlarge the Mapbox panel and add breathing room so the Attakar/Gura polygons read at a glance.

scene: 2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: Static rest frame with a non-destructive cursor implies but doesn't demonstrate the hover/publish interaction.
fix_recommendation: Capture a frame mid-hover on the map with a visible focus/tooltip state to evidence the interaction.

scene: 3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: Three competing vocabularies (Intervention/Comparison legend vs Attakar/Gura labels vs the single 'R6 — Attakar × Gura' checkbox) cause a brief 'what am I actually selecting?' hitch.
fix_recommendation: Unify the legend with the checklist — label the map swatches with the same names as the checklist option (or drop Intervention/Comparison and color the R6 study as one selectable unit), and add a '1 area selected' count near Submit.

scene: 3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: Map<->checklist two-way sync is the right pattern, but the legend semantics don't reconcile with the one checklist row and the copy promises 'wards' the map never renders.
fix_recommendation: Make the legend colors map 1:1 to checklist options, and either show ward sub-boundaries on hover/selection or remove the 'all of its wards together' promise from the helper text.

scene: 3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: 'Select a specific area from a checklist' is weakened by there being exactly one indivisible option, so firm-level territory self-selection is asserted more than shown.
fix_recommendation: Offer area-level options (e.g. Attakar and Gura as separately tickable studies, or sub-areas) so the checklist genuinely lets a firm express the specific territory it can staff.

scene: 3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: The enabled post-tick state is shown, but a mid-interaction cursor-over-checkbox artifact and no selection-count/enabled-state cue leave the gating behavior implied.
fix_recommendation: Capture a clean frame without the cursor occluding the checkbox, and add an explicit enabled-state cue (selection count or a 'ready to submit' hint).

scene: 4 — Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
detail: Three uncoordinated multicolor action icons (blue/green/brown), a large blank lower frame, and the cursor occluding the 'Award' label.
fix_recommendation: Unify the action icons into one consistent style (or monochrome with color only on hover), and reduce dead whitespace below the single row (response-count summary or tighter container).

scene: 4 — Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
detail: The Award-hover intent is captured, but the cursor obscures the exact action label it rests on.
fix_recommendation: Offset the captured cursor so it sits just left of (not over) the 'Award' label; add a visible hover-state highlight on the Award action.

scene: 5 — Maya awards the firm and sees it was notified
detail: Clean and unembarrassing but sparse: tall dead-space bands, a right-column gap, and 'R6'/'Round 6' + a lone-blue email-link consistency snag.
fix_recommendation: Tighten vertical rhythm, balance the right column, canonicalize the coverage-area label ('Round 6 (Attakar × Gura)' vs 'R6 — Attakar × Gura'), and fold the contact email into the navy palette.

scene: 5 — Maya awards the firm and sees it was notified
detail: Static confirmation reads cleanly but offers no completion cue or forward-motion affordance.
fix_recommendation: Add a completion cue (success-banner enter animation) and/or a disabled forward affordance on the deferred-step panel ('Stand up opportunity — coming soon') so it reads as a transition, not a dead end.
